target.name: Anysphere
target.founder: Michael Truell, Sualeh Asif, Aman Sanger, Arvid Lunnemark

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $9.7B - $300.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: OpenAI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $300B+

private.name: Anthropic
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $183B

private.name: Neuralink
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $9.7B

data.narrative:
Anysphere, a private-stage AI & Software company founded by Michael Truell, Sualeh Asif, Aman Sanger, and Arvid Lunnemark, has raised $3.3B+ and carries a current valuation of $29.3B. The relevant comp universe consists of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR), with primary focus on OpenAI, Anthropic, and Neuralink as same-sector, same-stage benchmarks.

The peer-median method is applicable and yields the primary valuation signal. Across 3 directly comparable private AI & Software companies, the median valuation is $183.0B, with a range spanning $9.7B (Neuralink) to $300.0B (OpenAI). This method reflects the substantial valuation dispersion within frontier AI, driven by differences in capital raised, commercialization progress, and perceived technical capability.

Triangulation across available methods supports a valuation range of $155.6B to $210.4B, with a median of $183.0B. The current valuation of $29.3B sits well below this range, suggesting either a meaningful discount to peer comparables or material uncertainty regarding Anysphere's competitive positioning relative to OpenAI and Anthropic. Key uncertainties include revenue visibility, product-market fit trajectory, and the sustainability of valuation multiples in a sector experiencing rapid capital inflows and shifting competitive dynamics. The limited methodological coverage (one applicable approach) reflects data constraints inherent to early-stage frontier technology.
